# Stacks excluded from terraform-ci validation. Tracked in Linear CZID-90.
# Parser does exact-match on each non-# line — keep BARE paths (no inline comments).

# ../../../modules/...): prod/downloads, prod/fivetran-ssh, staging/fivetran-ssh-nextgen
# now validate in CI.
#
# RESOLVED 2026-06-18 (CZID-217 — declared the missing s3_bucket_pipeline_public_assets
# variable): prod/batch now validates in CI.
#
# RESOLVED 2026-06-18 (CZID-218 — declared the missing idseq-prod remote-state data
# source + project_v1 variable, dropped during the idseq-infra import): prod/web now
# validates in CI.
#
# RESOLVED 2026-06-18 (CZID-216 — vendored ecs-cluster-v2.2.1 + its transitive
# sub-modules instance-cloud-init-script / aws-iam-policy-orgwide-secrets /
# aws-iam-policy-document-orgwide-secrets, repointed prod/ecs to the local copy):
# prod/ecs now validates in CI.
#
# The vendored ecs-cluster module dirs are consumed (relative ../ sub-module deps,
# template provider) — not standalone stacks — so they stay excluded from the flat
# "rest" bucket. They are still validated transitively via the stacks that use them
# (prod/ecs -> ecs-cluster-v2.2.1; ecs-service-with-alb is used by prod/web).
terraform/modules/ecs-cluster-v2.2.1
terraform/modules/ecs-cluster-v2.4.0
terraform/modules/ecs-service-with-alb-v0.421.0
#
# aws-eks-cluster (vendored v0.104.2-seqtoid.1, #403/#322): uses kubernetes/helm/kubectl
# providers configured only by the calling stack; standalone validate fails "Provider
# configuration not present". Validated transitively via {dev,staging,sandbox,prod}/eks.
terraform/modules/aws-eks-cluster-v0.104.2
